title: Convergence rates of Sum-of-Hermitian-Squares Hierarchies for the Pauli algebra
authors: Ali Almasi, Dávid Bugár, Cambyse Rouzé, Peter Brown
abstract: Moment/Sum-of-Hermitian-Squares relaxations for noncommutative polynomial optimization problems have become an important tool for analyzing problems within quantum theory. Despite their widespread success, little is known about their rate of convergence and, consequently, their accuracy. In this work, we develop explicit convergence rates for relaxations of noncommutative polynomial optimization problems generated from the Pauli algebra -- covering applications to the ground state energy problem for n-qubit systems. In particular, we show that the rate of convergence can be bounded in terms of the smallest roots of a family of orthogonal polynomials known as Krawtchouk polynomials. Our result represents the first quantitative analysis of the rate of convergence for relaxations of noncommutative polynomial optimization problems.
summary: This paper rigorously analyzes the convergence rate of Sum-of-Hermitian-Squares (SOHS) relaxations used to find the ground state energy of $n$-qubit systems. By extending techniques from commutative optimization to the non-commutative Pauli algebra, the authors derive an explicit error bound dependent on Krawtchouk polynomials. This provides a major quantitative advance in the mathematical analysis of quantum many-body problems.
main_problem: The paper develops explicit convergence rates for Sum-of-Hermitian-Squares (SOHS) relaxations applied to noncommutative polynomial optimization problems (NPO). This is crucial for accurately estimating ground state energies in quantum systems.
main_result: The authors provide a quantitative upper bound on the error, showing the convergence rate is governed by the smallest roots of Krawtchouk polynomials, representing the first such analysis for this class of problems.
method: The technique extends the polynomial kernel method from commutative SOS hierarchies to the non-commutative setting of the Pauli algebra, utilizing specialized linear maps $\Phi_\eta$ and properties derived from Krawtchouk polynomials.
model_or_system: The focus is on the ground state energy problem for $n$-qubit systems, formulated as an NPO problem over the Pauli algebra. The analysis uses the SOHS hierarchy, which generates a sequence of lower bounds ($H_r$) to the true minimum energy ($H_{\min}$).
key_observables: The primary observable is the error bound, $H_{\min} - H_r$, which is quantified in terms of the smallest root $\zeta_{n,r+1}$ of the Krawtchouk polynomial $K_{n,r+1}^{(4)}(x)$.
important_parameters: The parameters include the number of qubits ($n$), the level of the hierarchy ($r$), the maximum Pauli weight ($d$), and the Krawtchouk polynomial roots $\zeta_{n,r+1}$.
main_assumptions: The derivation relies on constructing a linear map $\Phi_\eta$ satisfying specific algebraic properties (P0)-(P3) within the Pauli algebra structure.
paper_structure: The paper builds by first establishing the necessary algebraic framework (Pauli algebra, diagonal maps $A_k, E_k$) using Krawtchouk polynomials, then deriving the required linear map $\Phi_\eta$ that satisfies the necessary properties, and finally applying this structure to derive the explicit convergence rate bound for the SOHS hierarchy.
why_interesting: This work provides rigorous mathematical tools to analyze the convergence of quantum many-body approximations, which is vital for developing reliable quantum simulation algorithms and understanding the limits of current optimization techniques for quantum chemistry and materials science.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
